seo.title: Building Customer Support with Agno + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with Agno + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building customer support agents with Agno enables powerful multi-modal RAG systems that integrate CRM data, knowledge bases, and tool-calling for ticket management and order lookups. However, these agents face risks like hallucinated responses, PII leaks, inconsistent tone, and policy violations that can damage customer trust and compliance. Vex provides essential guardrails through its verify decorator, ensuring response accuracy, security, and reliability at runtime.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with Agno
architectureOverview.description:
A typical Agno customer support agent follows a RAG pattern with three core layers: input processing, retrieval & reasoning, and action execution. User queries (text, images of tickets/receipts) flow through a multi-modal LLM that retrieves from vectorized knowledge bases (FAQs, product docs) and CRM systems (Zendesk, Salesforce). The agent uses tools for order lookup via API calls and ticket creation with structured outputs.

The agent's session memory maintains conversation context across multi-turn interactions, while team coordination handles escalations to human agents or specialists. Tool-calling ensures actions like 'create_ticket' generate valid JSON payloads that pass CRM schema validation. This architecture excels at handling complex support scenarios but amplifies risks around data accuracy and compliance.

Vex integration wraps the critical agent.run() call, verifying outputs against custom rules for PII redaction, tone analysis, policy checks, and escalation triggers before responses reach customers.

risks.name: Multi-modal agent misinterpreting image or audio inputs
risks.description: OCR errors on ticket images or misheard audio queries lead to wrong order lookups or escalations
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies extracted entities against CRM data and flags confidence < 90%

risks.name: Agentic session memory recalling fabricated past interactions
risks.description: Hallucinated conversation history causes inconsistent support or security breaches
risks.mitigation: Vex cross-checks memory references against actual CRM ticket history

risks.name: Structured output failing validation on complex nested schemas
risks.description: Invalid ticket creation JSON payloads rejected by CRM APIs, blocking resolutions
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validation + Pydantic enforcement before tool execution

risks.name: Team agent coordination producing contradictory sub-results
risks.description: Billing agent says 'refund approved' while escalation agent says 'under review'
risks.mitigation: Vex consistency checks across sub-agent outputs before final response

implementation.title: Basic Agno RAG Agent Setup
implementation.description: Initialize Agno agent with knowledge base and CRM tools for support workflows
implementation.code:
from agno.agent import Agent
from agno.tools.zendesk import ZendeskTools
from agno.retrievers import Vector
from agno.models.openai import OpenAI

agent = Agent(
    name="SupportBot",
    model=OpenAI(model="gpt-4o"),
    retriever=Vector.from_documents(docs=support_docs),
    tools=[ZendeskTools(api_key="your-key")],
    show_tool_calls=True
)

response = agent.run("Customer says order #12345 never arrived")
print(response)

implementation.title: Multi-modal Image Processing for Tickets
implementation.description: Handle customer-uploaded receipt images with vision capabilities
implementation.code:
from agno.agent import AgentMultiModal

vision_agent = AgentMultiModal(
    name="TicketVision",
    model=OpenAI(model="gpt-4o"),
    tools=[ZendeskTools()],
    max_tokens=2000
)

# Process uploaded image
result = vision_agent.run(
    "Extract order number and issue from this receipt image:",
    image_url="https://example.com/receipt.jpg"
)
print(result['order_number'])

implementation.title: Structured Ticket Creation Tool
implementation.description: Generate CRM-compliant ticket JSON with Pydantic validation
implementation.code:
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from agno.tools.base import Tool

class TicketSchema(BaseModel):
    customer_email: str = Field(..., description="Customer email")
    order_id: str
    issue_type: str
    priority: str = Field("medium", pattern="^(low|medium|high|critical)$")

class CreateTicketTool(Tool):
    name = "create_support_ticket"
    description = "Create Zendesk ticket from customer issue"
    schema = TicketSchema

    def call(self, **kwargs) -> dict:
        ticket = TicketSchema(**kwargs)
        return zendesk_client.tickets.create(ticket.dict())

agent.tools.append(CreateTicketTool())

implementation.title: Session Memory for Multi-turn Support
implementation.description: Maintain conversation context across support interactions
implementation.code:
from agno.memory.chat import ChatMemory

agent = Agent(
    name="SupportAgent",
    memory=ChatMemory.from_session_id("cust_123"),
    add_base_tools=True
)

# Multi-turn conversation
for message in customer_messages:
    response = agent.run(message)
    print(f"Bot: {response}")

# Memory persists across sessions
agent.save_memory()
